title: Standardize `next: NextAffordance[]` Affordance Hints Across All 30 .opencode/tools/
boundary: All `.opencode/tools/*.ts` plugin tool output JSON, plus `packages/opencode/src/tool/typed-result.ts` affordance computation
consumer_purpose: Model/TUI/auto-pilot consumers that read tool output JSON to suggest follow-on actions. The standardized `next` field makes tool output machine-actionable, replacing 10+ inconsistent ad-hoc affordance patterns (hint, next, recommendation) with one canonical contract.
claim_atoms: Extend NextAffordance interface with `when` and `args` fields (typed-result.ts:19)
Add 30 plugin tool IDs to toolCategory() with per-tool specificity (typed-result.ts:235)
Add per-tool affordance specs in affordancesAfterSuccess() with tool-ID-specific branches (typed-result.ts:269)
Add standardized `next: NextAffordance[]` to all 30 plugin tool JSON.stringify() returns in .opencode/tools/
Migrate 10+ ad-hoc hint/next/recommendation fields to the standard `next` array
Add bisect tests for schema validation, formatOutput/parseOutput roundtrip, and per-category affordance conformance

system_disconnect.description: Two parallel affordance systems exist but are disconnected: (a) typed-result.ts has NextAffordance, affordancesAfterSuccess(), wrapExecute() — used by built-in tools. (b) Plugin tools (.opencode/tools/) return raw JSON.stringify() strings with ad-hoc hint/next/recommendation fields. The bridge between them is registry.ts:fromPlugin() which normalizes plugin results to Tool.ExecuteResult — but the fromPlugin path never pulls affordances from the plugin's structured output into TypedToolResult.nextAffordances.
evidence: typed-result.ts:19-26 — NextAffordance interface exists
typed-result.ts:269-298 — affordancesAfterSuccess computes affordances centrally
typed-result.ts:425-449 — wrapExecute enriches Tool.ExecuteResult with typed results
session/tools.ts:108 — ALL tools (built-in + plugin) go through wrapExecute
registry.ts:328-362 — fromPlugin normalizes plugin ToolResult to Tool.ExecuteResult
registry.ts:358-360 — plugin output normalization: typeof result === 'string' ? result : result.output — discards extra fields
Zero .opencode/tools/*.ts files import from typed-result.ts or use wrapExecute
smart_delegate.ts:170-183 — ad-hoc 'next' string field
roadmap.ts:101 — ad-hoc 'recommendation' string field
fragment.ts:77-80 — ad-hoc 'hint' string fields
task_board.ts:251-256 — ad-hoc 'hint' string fields
smart_session.ts:74,160 — ad-hoc 'recommendation' string fields

tool_category_blindness.description: toolCategory() in typed-result.ts:235 uses substring prefix matching that doesn't recognize plugin tool IDs like smart_delegate, fragment, json_query. All 30 plugin tools fall through to the 'diagnose' category, receiving generic affordances. The function needs exact-match dispatch for plugin tool IDs before falling through to substring matching.
evidence: typed-result.ts:235-243 — toolCategory() only checks for 'read', 'write', 'edit', 'grep', 'search', 'find', 'bash', 'run', 'task', 'checkpoint', 'todo', 'plan', 'question'
Plugin tool IDs like 'smart_delegate', 'fragment', 'json_query', 'file_lock' match none of the above

ad_hoc_pattern_proven.description: 10+ tools already include affordance-like fields in their output — proving the pattern is natural and needed. But each tool invents its own schema (hint, next, recommendation) with no centralized contract. Standardizing to next: NextAffordance[] is the natural consolidation.

root_causes.hypothesis: Plugin tool results bypass affordance enrichment because fromPlugin() in registry.ts:358 only extracts output and metadata but discards any next/affordance fields from the plugin's structured return
evidence: registry.ts:358-360 — const output = typeof result === 'string' ? result : result.output; const metadata = typeof result === 'string' ? {} : (result.metadata ?? {}); — only output, metadata, and attachments survive
typed-result.ts:74-82 — makeSuccess calls affordancesAfterSuccess but only based on tool ID, not plugin-provided data
typed-result.ts:397-414 — enrichExecuteResult writes toolStatus, toolErrorKind, toolRecoverable, suggestedNextTools into metadata — but no affordance from plugin output

root_causes.hypothesis: toolCategory() cannot classify plugin tool IDs because it uses substring prefix matching designed for simple tool names (read, write, grep, bash), not compound plugin tool IDs (smart_delegate, json_query, fragment)
evidence: typed-result.ts:235-243 — substring matching only
All 30 plugin tools have compound names (smart_find, task_board, file_lock etc.) that don't match any substring pattern

root_causes.hypothesis: There is no canonical SuggestedAction type in the plugin SDK — plugin tool authors have no way to return structured affordances, so each tool invented its own ad-hoc field
evidence: packages/plugin/src/tool.ts:36-43 — ToolResult = string | { output, metadata?, attachments? } — no next field
10+ tools independently created hint/next/recommendation fields — same pattern, different names

design_decision.layer_system: Extend toolCategory() + affordancesAfterSuccess() in typed-result.ts to recognize plugin tool IDs. This gives every plugin tool category-based affordances automatically through the existing wrapExecute pipeline. No change needed in registry.ts or session/tools.ts.
design_decision.layer_tool: Add next: NextAffordance[] to each tool's JSON.stringify() output in .opencode/tools/. This puts tool-specific affordances directly in the visible output that the model and TUI read. These are additive to the system-level affordances.
design_decision.rationale: The system-level layer (wrapExecute) already works for all tools — it just needs tool ID recognition. The tool-level layer gives tool authors the ability to add context-specific suggestions (e.g., smart_find suggests reading specific found files). Both layers are advisory and consumers may ignore either or both.

fixes.description: Add `when` and `args` fields to the existing NextAffordance interface in typed-result.ts. Do NOT create a new SuggestedAction type — extend the canonical type per zoning-board recommendation.
risks: Adding optional fields is safe — formatOutput only reads action/target/command; new fields are silently serialized in JSON.stringify but not rendered in the human-readable section
Need to also update NextAffordanceSchema (typed-result.ts:54) with the new optional fields
validation: New fields appear in formatOutput JSON trailer

fixes.description: Extend `toolCategory()` with exact-match dispatch for all 30 plugin tool IDs, placed BEFORE the substring fallbacks. This ensures each plugin tool gets the correct category for affordance computation.
risks: New tools added later won't be in the exact-match map — they'll fall through to substring matching, which may misclassify them (e.g., 'smart_coordinate' contains neither 'read' nor 'write' etc. → 'diagnose'). Mitigation: add a comment and a CI check.
Substring fallbacks remain unchanged — no risk to built-in tools

fixes.description: Add per-tool-ID affordance branches in `affordancesAfterSuccess()` and `affordancesFor()` for tools with rich follow-on chains. Category-based defaults handle the rest.
fixes.after:
function affordancesAfterSuccess(toolID: string): NextAffordance[] {
  // Per-tool affordances (tool-specific chains)
  const toolAffordances: Record<string, NextAffordance[]> = {
    smart_find: [{ action: "read_source", command: "Read a found file", when: "Found files worth inspecting" }],
    smart_grep: [{ action: "read_source", command: "Read matching file", when: "Found matches worth inspecting" }],
    json_query: [{ action: "smart_grep", command: "Search for patterns in query results", when: "Want to trace references" }],
    file_lock: [{ action: "fragment", command: "Produce a fragment for editing", when: "Lock acquired — start editing" }],
    fragment: [{ action: "smart_batch", command: "Consolidate and apply fragments", when: "All fragments produced" }],
    smart_delegate: [{ action: "task_board", command: "Monitor fleet", when: "Want to see agent status" }],
    roadmap: [{ action: "smart_delegate", command: "Delegate work", when: "Ready to start a task" }],
    smart_bash: [{ action: "smart_bun", command: "Run bun for typecheck/tests", when: "Need verification" }],
    smart_bun: [{ action: "smart_edit", command: "Fix compilation errors", when: "Typecheck or tests failed" }],
    smart_edit: [{ action: "smart_bun", command: "Verify the edit", when: "Want to run typecheck/tests" }],
    smart_batch: [{ action: "smart_bun", command: "Verify the batch edit", when: "Want to run typecheck/tests" }],
    smart_session: [{ action: "smart_delegate", command: "Start next task", when: "Session initialized" }],
    task_board: [{ action: "smart_delegate", command: "Launch next wave", when: "Agents are idle" }],
    gate: [{ action: "smart_bun", command: "Run tests", when: "Checkpoint published" }],
    verify: [{ action: "smart_edit", command: "Fix verification failure", when: "Verification failed" }],
    smart_git: [{ action: "smart_bun", command: "Run typecheck", when: "After commit/push" }],
  }
  if (toolAffordances[toolID]) return toolAffordances[toolID]
  
  const cat = toolCategory(toolID)
  switch (cat) { ... }
}
risks: New tools need explicit mapping — without it they fall through to category defaults which may be too generic. Mitigation: CI test that verifies every known tool ID has non-empty affordances.
Some tool chains are speculative (smart_bash → smart_bun) — if wrong, the affordance is advisory; consumers can ignore it

per_tool_affordance_specs._note: Each tool gets two layers of affordances: (A) system-level from affordancesAfterSuccess based on toolCategory, (B) tool-level next array in the JSON output. The specs below describe (B) — what goes in the tool's own JSON output. Layer (A) is handled by fix-C above.

analytics.migration: No change — already no ad-hoc fields. Add next: [].

natural_next.hint: Read the file containing the finding

feedback.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint field, add next: [] (or next with smart_delegate if recoverable).

natural_next.hint: Produce a fragment for the locked file

fragment.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint strings, add next array with action-specific variants.

natural_next.hint: Search for references to queried keys
Delegate work to cartographer or architect

next_output.hint: Delegate to architect for plan design

natural_next.hint: Search for usages of read symbols

next_output.hint: Search for references to the read content

natural_next.hint: Search for usages of exported symbols

natural_next.when: Want to see all call sites

next_output.hint: Search for references to exported symbols

natural_next.hint: Run typecheck to catch compilation errors

replace_symbol.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint fields, add next array.

natural_next.hint: Delegate work to the appropriate agent

roadmap.migration: Replace recommendation string and ad-hoc next array with standardized next: NextAffordance[]. The roadmap.next action already returns a 'next' field (the action name), so the affordance field should be next_action or the action key should be renamed. RECOMMENDATION: rename the roadmap action-level 'next' field to 'items' to avoid collision with the affordance 'next'.

next_output.hint: Run typecheck or tests to validate

smart_batch.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint, add next array.

delegate.hint: Check fleet dashboard to monitor progress

send.hint: Check if recipient processed the message

smart_delegate.migration: Replace ad-hoc 'next' string and 'hint' strings with standardized next: NextAffordance[]. The existing 'next' field is a string hint (e.g., 'After cartographer completes, the next wave is architect') — preserve this text as the command/hint in the NextAffordance object.

next_output.hint: Run typecheck or tests to verify

smart_edit.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint strings, add next array.

next_output.hint: Read the contents of a found file

init.hint: Delegate to cartographer to map codebase

smart_session.migration: Replace recommendation and hint strings with standardized next: NextAffordance[].

natural_next.hint: Find files in large language directories

next_output.when: Want to explore a specific area
next_output.hint: Find files by pattern in large directories

smart_stats.migration: Remove ad-hoc hint, add next array.

ad_hoc_fields: hint (dynamic: stale agents, all done, next wave)

next_output.when: Agents are idle or wave incomplete
next_output.hint: Launch the next wave of agents

task_board.migration: Replace ad-hoc hint strings with action-specific next arrays.

rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order. Each phase is independently reversible: (1) Remove next fields from individual tools — tools still work. (2) Remove per-tool affordances from affordancesAfterSuccess() — falls back to category defaults. (3) Remove toolCategory() exact-match map — falls back to substring matching. (4) Remove new NextAffordance fields — they were optional additions; formatOutput ignores them.

risks_assessment.backward_compatibility: LOW. Adding optional fields to NextAffordance is safe. Adding next: NextAffordance[] to plugin tool JSON outputs is safe — fromPlugin() in registry.ts:358 only extracts output and metadata, and even if next appears at the top level, it's included in the string output which wrapExecute passes through as the TypedToolResult.data field.
risks_assessment.field_name_collision: MEDIUM. roadmap.ts already uses 'next' as an action name returning a 'next' field containing items. The roadmap/next action returns {action: 'next', next: [...items], recommendation: '...'}. The 'next' key at the output level is the list of roadmap items. Adding 'next' as affordances would collide. RECOMMENDATION: rename roadmap's items field to 'items' (not 'next') to free the 'next' key for affordances, OR use 'next_actions' as the affordance key in roadmap outputs.
risks_assessment.tool_category_fallback_accuracy: LOW. Plugin tools with novel IDs added later will fall through to 'diagnose' category, which provides generic affordances. Acceptable: affordances are advisory.
risks_assessment.merge_conflict_risk: LOW. All changes are within a single file (typed-result.ts phase 1) or isolated per-file (.opencode/tools/* phase 2). Parallel lane edits to the same tool file would need fragment coordination.
risks_assessment.performance: NEGLIGIBLE. Adding 1-3 NextAffordance objects to each tool output adds ~200-500 bytes of JSON. No measurable impact.

validation_strategy.regression_command: bun test --timeout 30000 test/tool/registry.test.ts test/session/prompt.test.ts test/session/message-v2.test.ts test/session/compaction.test.ts
smoke_steps: 2. bun test test/tool/typed-result.test.ts — all new tests pass
3. bun test test/tool/registry.test.ts — existing tests pass unchanged
4. bun test test/session/prompt.test.ts — output assertions still pass
6. Manual: invoke smart_find tool, verify next field in output
7. Manual: invoke smart_delegate tool, verify next field replaces ad-hoc next string

zoning_board_findings_addressed.resolution: Extended NextAffordance (not a new type). Added when and args to the existing canonical interface.
No affordances.ts shared module created. Each tool adds next inline. Centralized affordance logic stays in typed-result.ts.
Fix-C extends affordancesAfterSuccess() with per-tool-ID branches following the existing pattern.
